title_en: Hadiths on Commercial Law, Marriage, and Criminal Justice
title_id: Hadis-hadis tentang Hukum Dagang, Pernikahan, dan Peradilan Pidana
content_en:
The later sections of Sunan ad-Daraqutni address commercial law, family law, and criminal justice — areas where the schools of fiqh had developed significantly different positions and where the hadith evidence was correspondingly contested. Ad-Daraqutni's collection is particularly valuable here because it compiles not just the strongest narrations but all the narrations used in these legal debates.

In commercial law, the hadiths on the permissibility of various types of sale — forward sales, deferred payment sales, sales of indeterminate quantities — received different treatment from different schools. Ad-Daraqutni presents the full range of relevant narrations, often noting when a narration supports the Hanafi position but has a weak chain, or when a narration supports the Shafi'i position and has a strong chain. This information is directly useful for a legal scholar trying to determine which positions are most strongly supported by the hadith evidence.

The hadiths on marriage include contested questions about the guardian requirement, the minimum dower, and the conditions for valid divorce. Ad-Daraqutni's treatment of the hadiths about the guardian's role in marriage — one of the most contested issues between the Hanafi and Shafi'i schools — is extensive and includes all the major narrations with chain analysis.

For criminal law, the Sunan contains hadiths on the conditions for the hadd punishments — the evidentiary requirements, the categories of persons subject to the penalties, and the procedure for applying them. Ad-Daraqutni includes hadiths used by different schools to support their distinctive positions, along with his assessments of the relative strength of the different chains.

The sections on inheritance law contain hadiths that were used to resolve disputed questions about the shares of various heirs — particularly in cases involving maternal grandmothers, paternal relatives, or mixed situations not explicitly addressed by the Quranic verses on inheritance. Ad-Daraqutni's collection in this area preserves hadiths that are sometimes found only in his Sunan and are therefore of particular research value.
content_id:
Bagian-bagian akhir Sunan al-Daraqutni membahas hukum dagang, hukum keluarga, dan peradilan pidana — bidang-bidang di mana mazhab-mazhab fikih telah mengembangkan posisi yang sangat berbeda dan di mana bukti hadisnya pun turut diperdebatkan. Koleksi al-Daraqutni sangat berharga di sini karena menghimpun bukan hanya narasi-narasi terkuat, melainkan semua narasi yang digunakan dalam perdebatan hukum tersebut.

Dalam hukum dagang, hadis-hadis tentang kebolehan berbagai jenis jual beli — jual beli salam, jual beli dengan pembayaran tunda, jual beli barang yang jumlahnya tidak tentu — mendapat perlakuan berbeda dari mazhab yang berbeda. Al-Daraqutni menyajikan seluruh rangkaian narasi yang relevan, sering mencatat ketika suatu narasi mendukung posisi Hanafi tetapi memiliki sanad yang lemah, atau ketika suatu narasi mendukung posisi Syafi'i dan memiliki sanad yang kuat. Informasi ini secara langsung berguna bagi seorang sarjana hukum yang berusaha menentukan posisi mana yang paling kuat didukung oleh bukti hadis.

Hadis-hadis tentang pernikahan mencakup pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang diperdebatkan mengenai persyaratan wali, mahar minimum, dan syarat-syarat talak yang sah. Pembahasan al-Daraqutni tentang hadis-hadis mengenai peran wali dalam pernikahan — salah satu isu yang paling diperdebatkan antara mazhab Hanafi dan Syafi'i — sangat luas dan mencakup semua narasi utama beserta analisis sanad.

Untuk hukum pidana, Sunan ini berisi hadis-hadis tentang syarat-syarat penerapan hukuman hadd — persyaratan pembuktian, kategori orang yang terkena hukuman, dan prosedur penerapannya. Al-Daraqutni menyertakan hadis-hadis yang digunakan oleh berbagai mazhab untuk mendukung posisi khas mereka, disertai penilaiannya tentang kekuatan relatif dari sanad-sanad yang berbeda.

Bagian-bagian tentang hukum waris berisi hadis-hadis yang digunakan untuk menyelesaikan pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang diperdebatkan mengenai bagian berbagai ahli waris — khususnya dalam kasus yang melibatkan nenek dari pihak ibu, kerabat dari pihak ayah, atau situasi campuran yang tidak secara eksplisit dibahas oleh ayat-ayat Al-Qur'an tentang waris. Koleksi al-Daraqutni di bidang ini melestarikan hadis-hadis yang kadang hanya ditemukan dalam Sunannya dan oleh karena itu memiliki nilai penelitian yang khusus.
content_ar: The later sections of Sunan ad-Daraqutni address commercial law, الأسرة law, and criminal العدل — areas where the schools of الفقه had developed significantly different positions and where the الحديث evidence was correspondingly contested. Ad-Daraqutni's collection is particularly valuable here لأن it compiles not just the strongest narrations but all the narrations used in these legal debates. In commercial law, the hadiths on the permissibility of various types of sale — forward sales, deferred payment sales, sales of indeterminate quantities — received different treatment from different schools. Ad-Daraqutni presents the full range of relevant narrations, often noting عندما a narration supports the الحنفي position but has a weak chain, or عندما a narration supports the الشافعي position and has a strong chain. This information is directly useful for a legal scholar trying to determine which positions are most strongly supported by the الحديث evidence. The hadiths on marriage include contested questions about the guardian requirement, the minimum dower, and the conditions for valid divorce. Ad-Daraqutni's treatment of the hadiths about the guardian's role in marriage — one of the most contested issues between the الحنفي and الشافعي schools — is extensive and includes all the major narrations with chain analysis. For criminal law, the Sunan contains hadiths on the conditions for the hadd punishments — the evidentiary requirements, the categories of persons subject to the penalties, and the procedure for applying them. Ad-Daraqutni includes hadiths used by different schools to support their distinctive positions, along with his assessments of the relative القوة of the different chains. The sections on inheritance law contain hadiths that were used to resolve disputed questions about the shares of various heirs — particularly in cases involving maternal grandmothers, paternal relatives, or mixed situations not explicitly addressed by the القرآني الآيات on inheritance. Ad-Daraqutni's collection in this area preserves hadiths that are sometimes found only in his Sunan and are لذلك of particular research value.
